extraction.one_line_summary: LOPEZ Miguel Angel's Astana won team time trial by 2 seconds over Deceuninck-Quick Step, while Roglic's Jumbo-Visma crashed losing 40 seconds.
tactical_patterns: Astana beat Deceuninck by only 2 seconds despite DQS being sprint-specialized, suggesting course's technical corners favored bike-handling over raw power.
Jumbo-Visma's crash occurred on painted corners with water, revealing high-speed TTT cornering as major variance creator beyond pure watts.
EF Education First posted 14:58 with Van Garderen's TT prowess leading, showing non-GC teams can win TTTs when built around specialist.
Movistar finished only 16 seconds back with Quintana/Valverde sprinting each other at finish, exposing internal leadership tension despite team result.

rider_signals.ROGLIC Primoz: negative: Lost 40 seconds and sustained visible hip contusion in Jumbo-Visma crash, physical damage concern beyond time loss
rider_signals.LOPEZ Miguel Angel: positive: Astana won TTT making him first leader, team committed solely to his GC bid unlike Movistar's dual leadership
rider_signals.QUINTANA Nacho: positive: Led Movistar through finish showing Tour de France recovery complete, asserting team leadership claim over Valverde
rider_signals.VAN GARDEREN Tejay: positive: Powered EF to 14:58 third place despite non-GC team composition, confirms TT specialist value in stage-hunting squads
rider_signals.VALVERDE Alejandro: neutral: Sprinted Quintana for finish line despite irrelevant time gaps, revealing ego/leadership conflict within Movistar structure
